keyFindings.text: **Hallmark's checked-in reference inventory is larger and more layered than “~29 docs,” while its top-level reference count is exactly 24.** The 24 top-level files are: [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/anti-patterns.md:1] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/typography.md:1]

**Hallmark's curated-theme model complements rather than replaces sk-design's extracted corpus.** Hallmark uses 20 named themes plus a signal-gated custom branch and genre-scoped rotation; sk-design has 1,293 style directories and 1,290 canonical extracted bundles, while its interface mode treats corpus examples as bounded anchors rather than presets. The useful lesson is a small, curated decision layer or retrieval shortlist on top of corpus search—not copying 20 theme presets wholesale into a system that deliberately resists chooser/preset behavior. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md:38] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md:240] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/SKILL.md:86] [INFERENCE: based on the checked-in `.opencode/skills/sk-design/styles/` inventory of 1,293 directories and 1,290 `*-canonical.json` files]

**License verdict: direct copying and adaptation are permitted, including redistribution and sublicensing, but copied or substantially reused Hallmark material must retain the Hallmark copyright and MIT permission notice.** The license explicitly grants use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and sale rights, then conditions copies or substantial portions on including the copyright and permission notice. A clean sk-design adoption therefore has two safe lanes: (a) `COPY`/close `ADAPT` with the MIT notice preserved in the affected vendored or attribution surface, or (b) `LEARN`/independent re-expression where no Hallmark text or substantial structure is copied. The warranty disclaimer should travel with copied/substantial portions as part of the full notice. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/LICENSE:1] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/LICENSE:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/LICENSE:12]

**Several Hallmark roadmap items already exist in sk-design, which changes their verdict from “new” to “gap-fill.”** sk-design already has a redesign lane, variation-set procedure and variant parameter contract, foundations data-viz guidance, live-site/code-backed md-generator extraction, and a large styles corpus. Candidate net-new or materially additive ideas are theme-aware motion tokens, stronger structural cookbook selection, brand-first authoring into a portable system, and an image-generation enrichment hook; each still needs detailed overlap analysis before adoption. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/ROADMAP.md:15] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/ROADMAP.md:17] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/ROADMAP.md:19] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/ROADMAP.md:25] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/ROADMAP.md:29] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/SKILL.md:80] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/SKILL.md:28] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/assets/variant-parameter-contract.md:15]

**The canonical Hallmark contract is four verbs, but sk-design already covers all four jobs without needing a sixth mode.** Hallmark defines default build, read-only audit, boundary-preserving redesign, and screenshot/URL study with optional portable `design.md` emission. sk-design routes invention/reshape/redesign to `interface`, static systems to `foundations`, critique to `audit`, interaction choreography to `motion`, and measured `DESIGN.md` extraction to `md-generator`; the canonical commands are `/interface:design`, `/interface:foundations`, `/interface:motion`, `/interface:audit`, and `/interface:design-reference`. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md:19] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md:25] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:25] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:36]

**The supplied “57 gates” figure is stale inside Hallmark itself.** `README.md` still says 57, but the canonical `slop-test.md` title says 58 and the operative `SKILL.md` preview/build contract repeatedly requires 58/58. Later gate comparison should therefore use `slop-test.md` as authority and treat README's 57 as documentation drift. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/README.md:13] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:1] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md:419] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md:470]

**The top-level list is not the complete recursive knowledge surface.** Hallmark also contains two verb packets (`audit`, `redesign`), 21 per-macrostructure packets, and 50 per-component packets, selected load-on-demand by `SKILL.md`; these subordinate catalogs need their own later matrix coverage rather than being collapsed into the 24 top-level rows. Hallmark explicitly instructs loading one chosen macrostructure file and only the chosen component archetypes, which is a useful context-budget pattern for sk-design's procedure-card system. **Verb-to-mode map and immediate verdicts:** [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md:25] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:25]

**Fifteen Hallmark checks expose genuinely absent named probes worth adding, with seven as the highest-value first tranche.** The first tranche is: invented metrics (46), re-drawn UI chrome (47), AI-nav fingerprint (42), AI-footer fingerprint (43), tag-left/header-right section heads (54), compound hover effects (13), and focus-ring animation (15). The next tranche is side-stripe cards (5), redundant success toasts (16), tooltip delay asymmetry (17), missing carousel pause (18), placeholder/startup cliché copy (19), default-Lottie enrichment (31), overused outlier type (38), and italic display heads (38a). These are missing as named, checkable rules even where a broad critique lens could notice them. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:32] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:48] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:50] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:132] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:146] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:152] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:180]

**Hallmark's audit verb is otherwise a smaller version of sk-design's existing findings-first contract.** Tell/Where/Severity/Fix maps to Observation/Evidence/Category/Problem/Fix/Owner, and both are read-only. Hallmark's useful additions are structural-fingerprint checking, declared-system drift checking, and genre-aware exceptions; sk-design already has stronger DESIGN.md/token-source drift and register-sensitive severity, so only structural fingerprint and applicable-context exception fields need adaptation. The Hallmark-specific CSS stamp requirement should be skipped. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/verbs/audit.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/verbs/audit.md:10] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/verbs/audit.md:12] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/verbs/audit.md:18] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/audit-contract.md:106] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/anti-patterns-production.md:51]

**Hallmark's severity labels conflict with sk-design's severity semantics and must not be copied.** Hallmark calls a structural template “critical” because it ships as slop and “major” because it looks AI-generated; sk-design reserves P0/P1 for task failure, blocked access, serious usability/accessibility failures, or systemic harm, while a single tell is normally P2/P3. The right adaptation is to add Hallmark-derived tells to the Anti-Patterns dimension and let evidence/user impact determine P0-P3. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/verbs/audit.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/verbs/audit.md:7] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/audit-contract.md:36] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/ai-fingerprint-tells.md:40]

**Several precise Hallmark implementation gates should become static-risk findings, not universal design laws.** The useful outcomes are no horizontal overflow, hero content visible at a target laptop viewport, stable form geometry, clickable labels that do not wrap, image grids that can shrink, long display text that survives mobile, and sticky layers that do not collide. Hallmark's prescribed fixes or constants—overflow-x: clip on every page, bottom hero padding at least 1.3× top, zero-chroma bans, three-font and two-outlier ceilings—are context-sensitive and can conflict with brand, platform, or content. Verdict: LEARN from the failure modes; ADAPT outcome checks into responsive/production references; SKIP hard-coded remedies as global blockers. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:90] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:98] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:106] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:136] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:172] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:184] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/anti-patterns-production.md:101]

**sk-design already covers the release-critical half of Hallmark and is materially stronger outside presentation taste.** Existing contracts cover generic gradients/cards, harsh black/white, token and spacing drift, interaction states, reduced motion, contrast, decorative accessibility, responsive failure, Core Web Vitals, evidence labels, and measured severity. Hallmark adds specificity around recognizable page chrome and narrow CSS failure patterns, but it does not replace keyboard, screen-reader, zoom/reflow, assistive-tech, performance-metric, or evidence-coverage contracts. [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/procedures/ai-slop-check.md:24] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/anti-patterns-production.md:49] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/anti-patterns-production.md:101] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/accessibility-performance.md:38] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/accessibility-performance.md:94] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/audit-contract.md:59]

**The best surgical implementation is to extend the existing fingerprint pipeline, not create a Hallmark checklist fork.** Named presentation tells belong in design-audit/references/ai-fingerprint-tells.md, mirrored into assets/ai-fingerprint-registry.json, assets/ai-fingerprint-self-defect-card.md, and tell/clean fixtures; behavioral and responsive risks belong in anti-patterns-production.md or accessibility-performance.md; the six-lens preflight belongs in the shared polish card. This preserves the shipped procedure selection, evidence requirements, machine parity checks, and owner routing. [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/ai-fingerprint-tells.md:22] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/ai-fingerprint-tells.md:26] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/procedures/ai-slop-check.md:33] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/procedures/polish-gate-orchestration.md:29]

**The six-axis pre-emit critique is a compact improvement to the shared polish gate, especially its Philosophy, Specificity, Restraint, and Variety axes.** sk-design already has explicit hierarchy, execution/polish, intentionality, and restraint coverage, but no single preflight that asks whether the artifact has a position, fits this brief, removes non-earning decoration, and avoids repeating a prior structural fingerprint. Verdict: ADAPT the six concepts into one evidence row in shared/procedures/polish-gate-orchestration.md, using sk-design language and evidence labels; SKIP Hallmark's stamp comment and mandatory numeric score because audit does not mutate source and a number without calibration would imply false precision. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:9] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:15] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/anti-slop-principles.md:34] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/anti-slop-principles.md:41] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/critique-hardening.md:107] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/procedures/polish-gate-orchestration.md:37]

**The useful unit of reuse is a selective probe library, not Hallmark's 58/58 all-or-nothing gate.** Hallmark requires every answer to be “no” and mixes genre exceptions into the same checklist; sk-design instead treats tells as evidence-backed hypotheses, keeps legitimate brand choices, and grades user impact through P0-P3 plus five audit dimensions. Verdict: ADAPT individual probes into existing references and the machine-readable fingerprint registry; SKIP wholesale checklist replacement. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/slop-test.md:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/procedures/ai-slop-check.md:35] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/audit-contract.md:36]

**Attestation belongs in provenance and eligibility, not portable style prose.** Hallmark separates diagnosis permission from portable emission and records source, date, attestation, and confidence. sk-design's style_provenance is stronger: source/original/screenshot URLs, capture time, license status, rights-known, and evidence scope; exact reuse requires known provenance and allowed rights. Add extractionAuthorization = user-owned | permitted-public-reference | analysis-only at intake and map it to provenance/evidence scope and reuse eligibility. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:447] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:471] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/styles/_db/schema.mjs:97] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/styles/_db/retrieval.mjs:153]

**Hallmark study is a useful compact diagnosis layer, not a replacement extractor.** Its image/URL modes run surface, type, structure, motion, and rhythm passes, then separate diagnosis, build, and portable-file emission. sk-design already crawls rendered pages at five viewports, captures computed styles and interaction states, and runs accessibility, dark-mode, framework, icon, motion, and boundary detectors. ADAPT a concise measured DNA preview into /interface:design-reference confirm presentation; do not add another public verb or backend. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:11] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:106] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/feature-catalog/extract/extract.md:20] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/feature-catalog/extract/extract.md:51]

**Hallmark's compact axes complement full style bundles.** paper band/hue, accent hue/footprint, type roles/pairing, density, asymmetry, treatments, reveal, and macrostructure form a useful query vocabulary. The database already indexes facets, capabilities, token axes, sections, FTS, and vectors. Derive low-cardinality facets during indexing—paper-band, accent-hue, accent-footprint, density, asymmetry, treatment, motion-stance, macrostructure-family—from canonical measured data. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:225] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:237] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/styles/_db/schema.mjs:125] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/styles/_db/retrieval.mjs:172]

**Measured extraction and brand-first authoring must remain separate contracts.** Hallmark can emit the same compact file after either a user-approved build or a study. sk-design routes briefs without measurable sources to interface aesthetic direction, preventing authored values from masquerading as extraction. LEARN from Hallmark's opt-in lock moment, but implement future authoring through design-interface plus design-foundations with originClass = authored; never register it as measured corpus evidence. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/design-md.md:19] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/design-md.md:114] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/procedures/design-system-extraction.md:35] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/procedures/design-system-extraction.md:41]

**Portable token/export coverage mostly exists; inline bundling is a regression.** Hallmark emits tokens.css, Tailwind v4, DTCG JSON, and shadcn variables inline on managed projects. A sk-design bundle already has DESIGN.md, css-variables.css, tailwind-v4.css, design-tokens.json, canonical JSON, and source.md. SKIP duplicates inside DESIGN.md. INSPIRE-NEW only an opt-in shadcn sibling adapter when framework detection confirms need. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/export-formats.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/export-formats.md:320] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/styles/099-supply/source.md:12]

**Role versus observation should be explicit; screenshot estimates must stay outside measured extraction.** Hallmark separates type roles from faces and color bands from exact values. URL mode fills exact fields; image mode uses nulls or labeled estimates. sk-design requires every rendered value verbatim from tokens.json and forbids filling absent data. Add originClass = measured | inferred and sourceMode to field evidence where missing; reserve estimated bands for a future authored lane. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:219] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:241] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/references/design-md-format.md:27] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/references/design-md-format.md:43]

**The strongest reusable schema addition is optional composition DNA.** Hallmark explicitly records macrostructure, alternate structure, hero/pitch/nav/footer archetypes, knobs, density, asymmetry, and treatments. v3 has rich Layout and Components prose but no compact queryable tuple. Add optional compositionDNA to backend/scripts/schema-v3.ts and types.ts with macrostructure, regions, variationKnobs, density, asymmetry, treatments, and evidence labels; render it through existing Layout/Components rather than importing Hallmark's archetype codes. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:210] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/study.md:218] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/references/design-md-format.md:157] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/references/design-md-format.md:206]

**v3 omits motion despite already measuring it.** Hallmark's portable file has Motion stance plus easing/duration tokens; study records reveal and motion_library. sk-design extracts transition/animation durations, easing, and choreography, but DESIGN.md and Quick Start omit a motion section. Add conditional Motion to design-md-format.md and the formatter, with observed libraries, primitives, durations, easings, choreography, and reduced-motion evidence; emit Quick Start motion tokens only when measured. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/design-md.md:82] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/design-md.md:93] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/feature-catalog/extract/extract.md:55] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/references/design-md-format.md:235]

**Focus timing and geometry deserve a surgical hardening.** Hallmark requires focus rings to appear instantly, forbids animated focus-ring opacity/transform, gates hover behind hover-capable media, and keeps border width constant across input states to prevent layout shift. sk-design's focus card currently allows "instant or micro-feedback tier," which is weaker, although its hover card already has strong `(hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)` gating. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/interaction-and-states.md:70] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/interaction-and-states.md:111] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/assets/motion-pattern-cards.md:60] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/assets/motion-pattern-cards.md:77] **Verdict: LEARN.** Change the focus card timing to instant, add "no animated focus-ring appearance," and add layout-stable state geometry to the interaction-state proof gate. Value: high. Effort: low.

**Hallmark supplies a complete three-role easing set; sk-design names several enter curves but leaves exit and toggle curves underspecified.** Hallmark defines enter, exit, and symmetric-toggle curves as named tokens. sk-design already contains Hallmark's enter curve exactly as `--ease-out-expo`, recommends ease-in for exits, and permits springs only for physical settle, but it does not provide canonical exit or toggle token values. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/motion.md:15] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:69] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/references/motion-strategy.md:57] **Verdict: ADAPT.** Add role-based `--ease-enter`, `--ease-exit`, and `--ease-toggle` examples to `design-motion/references/motion-strategy.md`; alias the existing expo curve for entry and add explicit exit/toggle curves. Because close curve reuse would copy Hallmark values, preserve Hallmark's MIT copyright and permission notice in the chosen attribution surface. Value: medium-high. Effort: low.

**Hallmark's microinteraction recipes add latency and feedback policy that sk-design's generic cards do not yet encode.** Hallmark distinguishes silent visible success from toast-worthy hidden failure, favors optimistic update with rollback/Undo for reversible actions, gives hover versus focus tooltip delays, prevents spinner flashes with delayed/minimum visibility, keeps command-palette open and keyboard movement instant, and specifies search debounce/announcement behavior. sk-design has stronger generic pattern cards and a full async state-machine card, but its microinteraction reference only broadly covers loading, gestures, delight, and icon morphing. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:9] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:126] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:171] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:186] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/references/micro-interactions.md:110] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/assets/motion-pattern-cards.md:200] **Verdict: LEARN.** Add a "latency and feedback policy" section to `design-motion/references/micro-interactions.md`, then add opt-in recipe cards for tooltip, optimistic rollback, search-as-you-type, and command palette to `design-motion/assets/motion-pattern-cards.md`. Keep the async state-machine card as the structural owner instead of cloning recipe code. Value: high. Effort: medium.

**Hallmark's theme-aware multiplier is useful as a capability idea, but its named-theme table should not be copied into sk-design.** Hallmark maps ten named themes to `0x-1.3x` duration scales, easing flavor, and spring permission. sk-design already has a more general Brand-vs-Product motion budget that distinguishes one expressive Brand entrance from Product-only state transitions, but it has no portable token for motion character inside a theme or style bundle. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:223] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/register.md:49] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/register.md:73] **Verdict: INSPIRE-NEW.** Add an optional semantic `motionCharacter` contract owned by Foundations and consumed by Motion: `durationScale`, `easingRole`, `springPolicy`, `entrancePolicy`, `maxConcurrentPrimitives`, and `reducedMotionPolicy`. Add the fields to `design-foundations/assets/token-starter.md`, define the vocabulary in `shared/design-token-vocabulary.md`, and carry them in motion cards/handoff. Do not hardcode Hallmark theme names or copy exact multipliers; derive values from owned brand evidence and the register. Value: high. Effort: medium.

**sk-design's material model is safer and more capable than Hallmark's absolute transform/opacity rule.** Hallmark says only transform and opacity may animate, yet its own accordion recipe animates `grid-template-rows`, and its microinteraction recipes allow color, border, blur, shadow, and value animation. sk-design instead classifies composite, paint, and layout costs; permits bounded paint, clip/mask, and FLIP/grid-row cases; forbids raw scroll loops and `transition: all`; and requires measurement before performance claims. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/motion.md:9] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/motion.md:81] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:202] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/references/performance-reduced-motion.md:35] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/references/motion-strategy.md:79] **Verdict: SKIP** the absolute ban and retain sk-design's cost-tier model. **Verdict: LEARN** from Hallmark's small-distance examples by adding semantic `amplitude/travel` and `stagger-cap` vocabulary to `shared/design-token-vocabulary.md`, without promoting `-1px`, `8px`, or `0.96` to universal laws. Value: medium. Effort: low.

**sk-design's reduced-motion default is stricter and more internally consistent, while Hallmark contributes a useful semantic classification.** Hallmark's `motion.md` globally forces `150ms`, says functional loops continue more slowly, and says spatial movement becomes opacity; elsewhere `microinteractions.md` allows skip or `0.01s`. sk-design consistently uses `0.01ms`, zero duration, instant/static state, color/focus, or opacity while requiring the same state signal and pausing off-screen loops. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/motion.md:83] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:37] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:242] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/references/performance-reduced-motion.md:78] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/assets/motion-pattern-cards.md:94] **Verdict: SKIP** Hallmark's global `150ms` override. **Verdict: LEARN** from its semantic distinction: spatial/decorative motion becomes instant or static, optional short opacity may remain when safe, and functional status must persist without depending on motion. Encode that classification in `design-motion/references/performance-reduced-motion.md` and require each pattern card to name which branch it uses. Value: medium-high. Effort: low.

**The most concrete state gap is inside sk-design's procedure card, not its vocabulary.** Hallmark's eight-state checklist requires default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, and success. sk-design's shared vocabulary is actually broader because it also names selected, but `design-motion/procedures/interaction-states-pass.md` only requires default, hover, active, disabled, focus, and loading in its output contract and procedure. Error, success, and selected can therefore disappear between vocabulary and handoff. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/interaction-and-states.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/design-token-vocabulary.md:81] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/procedures/interaction-states-pass.md:17] **Verdict: ADAPT.** Extend `interaction-states-pass.md` to an applicable-state matrix covering default, hover, focus-visible, active/pressed, selected, disabled, loading/pending, error, success, and empty when relevant. Do not require every state on every element; require an explicit N/A with reason. Value: high. Effort: low.

**The timing systems overlap, but Hallmark names two useful gaps: truly instant behavior and latency/disclosure delays.** Hallmark's detailed canon separates `0ms`, `80-120ms`, `150-200ms`, `250-300ms`, and `400-500ms`; it explicitly assigns focus, keyboard navigation, and error appearance to `0ms`, while tooltip hover delay, search debounce, dwell time, and minimum spinner visibility are modeled separately from transition duration. sk-design has strong `100-150ms`, `200-300ms`, `300-500ms`, and `500-800ms` bands, plus a Product `150-250ms` budget, but it does not define an instant tier or a separate delay/latency vocabulary. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:55] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:138] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/microinteractions.md:186] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-motion/references/motion-strategy.md:46] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/numeric-design-laws.md:26] **Verdict: LEARN.** Add an advisory `motion-instant` row (`0ms` for focus indication, keyboard selection, and error appearance) and distinguish transition duration from disclosure delay, debounce, dwell, and minimum-visible time in `shared/numeric-design-laws.md` and `design-motion/references/motion-strategy.md`. Value: high. Effort: low.

**Hallmark usefully connects headline copy length to display sizing, but its English character buckets and global font ceilings are too rigid.** Hallmark steps display size down beyond 50 and 90 characters and caps most display text, while sk-design already bounds fluid type, balances short headings, checks localization, and hard-fails overlong rendered hero line counts. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/typography.md:190] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/typography.md:192] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/type/typography-system.md:58] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/type/typography-system.md:90] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/mechanical-defaults.md:45] **Verdict: LEARN.** Add a content-responsive display rule to `typography-system.md`: verify rendered line count, longest unbreakable segment, localization expansion, and minimum viewport; step down the role when the copy breaks the composition. Keep character counts as English-language examples, not laws. Value: high. Effort: low.

**Hallmark's color model mostly confirms sk-design rather than extending it; its best transferable detail is the distinction between opaque palette roles and alpha modifiers.** Both systems prefer OKLCH, tint neutrals toward project evidence, assign semantic roles before values, rebuild dark surfaces through lightness, desaturate accents in dark mode, and reject color-only meaning. Hallmark additionally states that named palette colors should be opaque and transparency should be a modifier for overlays and shadows. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/color.md:7] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/color.md:82] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/color/palette-theming.md:52] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/color/palette-theming.md:81] **Verdict: ADAPT.** Add an opacity-modifier rule to `design-foundations/references/color/palette-theming.md` and `design-foundations/assets/token-starter.md`: canonical canvas/surface/text/accent/signal tokens are opaque; overlay, scrim, shadow, and optical-separator tokens may carry alpha and must name that role. Value: medium. Effort: low.

**Hallmark's core copy rules are already present, often more completely, in sk-design; the remaining gap is latency-aware loading copy.** Both require specific verbs, direct recovery, active voice, stable action names, useful empty states, no generic filler, and no humor in frustration paths. sk-design additionally governs fake precision, localization expansion, mock data, imagery seeds, and one register. Hallmark distinguishes silent short waits, labeled medium waits, and progress plus an honest expectation for long waits. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/copy.md:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/copy.md:21] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/copy.md:39] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/copy-and-mock-data.md:41] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/copy-and-mock-data.md:119] **Verdict: LEARN.** Add a loading-state formula to `copy-and-mock-data.md`: suppress transient text for genuinely short waits, name the operation for perceptible waits, and provide real progress or an honest expectation for long waits. Derive thresholds from measured product latency rather than importing `2s/10s` as universal cutoffs. Value: medium-high. Effort: low. **Verdict: SKIP** copying Hallmark's voice-sample quotations: many are attributed third-party lines, so Hallmark's MIT license does not establish redistribution rights for those quotations. Value: high defensive value. Effort: none.

**Hallmark's fixed color doctrine should not replace sk-design's register- and evidence-aware dosage model.** Hallmark requires OKLCH only, one accent at roughly 3-5 percent, no pure black or white, and forbids several gradient families. sk-design instead prefers OKLCH for new systems while requiring gamut fallbacks, lets restrained Product surfaces use rare accent, and lets earned Brand surfaces use committed, full-palette, or drenched color. That is the more general contract. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/color.md:7] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/color.md:73] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/color/oklch-workflow.md:48] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/color/palette-theming.md:35] **Verdict: SKIP** Hallmark's universal percentages and bans. **Verdict: LEARN** by adding an audit prompt that asks whether accent footprint is proportionate to the declared register and backed by a role, without enforcing a fixed percentage. Target `design-audit/procedures/ai-slop-check.md` and `design-foundations/references/color/palette-theming.md`. Value: medium-high. Effort: low.

**Hallmark's layout fundamentals are already covered, and its universal asymmetry claim conflicts with sk-design's calibrated variance model.** Both systems use a spacing scale, Grid for page structure, Flexbox for component internals, intrinsic `auto-fit/minmax()` grids, named areas, breakouts, and anti-card-grid rules. Hallmark says symmetry reads as generated; sk-design explicitly allows calm symmetry when the brief resolves to low variance and treats asymmetry as a choice that never licenses breakage. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/layout-and-space.md:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/layout-and-space.md:35] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/layout/layout-responsive.md:73] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/brief-to-dials.md:107] **Verdict: SKIP** universal asymmetry, fixed shadow recipes, and copied z-index values. **Verdict: LEARN** by adding a scoped overflow recipe to `layout-responsive.md`: image tracks use `minmax(0, 1fr)`, long display text gets a wrap strategy, and intentional breakout regions prove no document-level horizontal scroll without masking unrelated overflow. Value: high. Effort: low.

**Responsive coverage needs a proof matrix, not Hallmark's device list as doctrine.** sk-design has the stronger conceptual model: content-driven breakpoints, container queries for components, pointer/hover capability queries, orientation, safe areas, and context adaptation. Hallmark adds an operational pre-ship sweep at 320, 375, 414, and 768 CSS pixels plus checks for wrapped affordances, image-grid overflow, long words, per-theme mobile overrides, and focus-induced scroll jumps. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/responsive.md:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/responsive.md:19] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/layout/layout-responsive.md:103] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/layout/layout-responsive.md:120] **Verdict: ADAPT.** Add a `verificationViewportSet` and responsive-risk table to the `/interface:foundations` visible artifact and Foundations handoff: minimum supported width, each content-driven transition width, one intermediate width, zoom/text expansion, pointer/hover modes, orientation, safe areas, horizontal overflow, unbreakable content, and interactive-label wrapping. Use Hallmark's four widths only as a web-marketing default example. Add corresponding findings to `design-audit/references/anti-patterns-production.md`. Value: high. Effort: medium.

**The highest-value adoption is Hallmark's trigger-gated tuned-versus-bespoke authoring branch, translated to sk-design's precedent corpus rather than a 20-theme catalog.** Hallmark only surfaces custom authoring when the brief supplies a named brand color, a multi-attribute off-catalog mood, a brand-mood artifact, or a singular structural vision; tuned custom changes palette and type while retaining structure, whereas bespoke changes the composition but keeps the quality floor. sk-design has a one-question ambiguity rule, pinned-axis precedence, an aesthetic-direction procedure, and a large extracted style corpus, but no equivalent authored-branch classification. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/custom-theme.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/custom-theme.md:16] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/custom-theme.md:50] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/brief-to-dials.md:48] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/procedures/aesthetic-direction.md:21] **Verdict: ADAPT.** Extend `design-interface/procedures/aesthetic-direction.md` and the `/interface:foundations` brief with `precedent-grounded | authored-tuned | authored-bespoke`. Trigger authored-tuned for explicit brand tokens or a multi-attribute direction unsupported by retrieved evidence; trigger authored-bespoke only when structure itself is the ask. Ask one focused question at the real fork, preserve pinned axes and existing systems, label every authored value as authored rather than measured, preview palette/type/structure before implementation, and run the same audit floor. Do not create a permanent preset or copy Hallmark's named-theme axes. Value: very high. Effort: medium.

**Typography has one concrete implementation-handoff gap: sk-design asks for metric-matched fallbacks but does not name the metric override fields.** Hallmark requires `size-adjust`, `ascent-override`, `descent-override`, and `line-gap-override`; sk-design currently says to choose metric-matched fallbacks and use `font-display`, but its token starter has no fallback-metrics row. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/typography.md:213] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/type/typography-system.md:72] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/assets/token-starter.md:81] **Verdict: ADAPT.** Extend `design-foundations/assets/token-starter.md` type rows with primary family, fallback family, `font-display`, `size-adjust`, ascent, descent, and line-gap overrides; add a proof item to `typography-system.md` that fallback and loaded layouts do not shift materially. Value: high. Effort: low.

**`assets.md` adds useful source-choice and delivery checks, but its vendor canon and numeric taste rules are not durable.** Adapt the source-order questions—does the brief need media, is an owned asset available, can the needed subject be produced safely, what is the fallback—and add explicit `license`, `attribution`, `trademark/personalityRights`, `transformation`, and `sourceURL` fields to the existing image evidence pass. Also add hero-video proof for poster, no autoplay audio, reduced-motion/static alternative, and LCP loading behavior when applicable. Skip vendor rankings, claimed current prices, exact icon sizes, universal photo tilts, and copyright labels copied from the catalogue; those are time-sensitive and sometimes inaccurate enough to require primary-source verification. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/assets.md:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/assets.md:21] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/assets.md:240] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/assets/interface-preflight-card.md:114] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/creation-contract.md:145]

**`custom-craft.md` shows the current SVG prohibition is over-broad, while its technology hierarchy is too doctrinaire to copy.** A crude hand-rolled scene used because no asset exists should fail, but a brief-grounded, deliberately designed CSS/SVG illustration with semantic labeling, a reduced-motion fallback, bounded size, and render proof is different. Amend `copy-and-mock-data.md` and the preflight card to ban `unvalidated decorative SVG fallback`, not all hand-built SVG; route deliberate custom craft through the real-UI loop with an asset evidence record (`purpose`, `owned/generated/source`, `bundleCost`, `fallback`, `altOrDecorative`, `renderProof`). Keep reuse-before-generate when an owned system already supplies the asset, and do not universalize Hallmark's A-through-F medium ordering or its specific tool vendors. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/custom-craft.md:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/custom-craft.md:83] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/custom-craft.md:304] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/copy-and-mock-data.md:167] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/real-ui-loop.md:57]

**Hallmark's sampled macrostructure packets prove the schema scales across marketing, workflow, and spatial-information pages, but several recipes conflict with sk-design gates.** Workbench requires real product captures, Narrative Workflow is justified only by a real sequence, and Map/Diagram makes spatial topology the primary composition. Those are useful selection heuristics. Conversely, Workbench's inline annotation arrows conflict with Hallmark's own later numbered-pin preference, and its CSS-art product mockup example conflicts with sk-design's ban on div-based fake screenshots. Record these patterns as intent-level recipes and preserve sk-design's evidence gate; do not import their sample markup as canonical implementation. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/macrostructures/05-workbench.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/macrostructures/14-narrative-workflow.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/macrostructures/19-map-diagram.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/copy-and-mock-data.md:163]

**Hallmark's strongest structural contribution is a whole-page choice before component styling, not its list of 21 names.** `macrostructures.md` binds heading placement, body composition, dividers, controls, imagery, reveal, navigation, and footer into a single page fingerprint, then loads only the selected packet. sk-design already rejects the median at candidate index 0 and asks wireframes to differ structurally, but it does not require a named page-shape record that carries regions, proof placement, navigation/footer treatment, and responsive collapse into handoff. Adapt the idea into `design-interface/references/design-process/variation-diversity.md` and `procedures/wireframe-exploration.md`: each candidate should declare `pageShape`, `regions`, `proofPlacement`, `navFooterTreatment`, `responsiveCollapse`, and `signatureMove`; load one matching private recipe only after selection. Do not copy all Hallmark names or its CSS stamp. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/macrostructures.md:3] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/macrostructures.md:25] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/variation-diversity.md:42] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/procedures/wireframe-exploration.md:25]

**Hero enrichment exposes a direct sk-design contract bug: mandatory media can itself be the AI default.** Hallmark defaults to typography-only, requires media to answer a brief signal, and uses a tiered source decision only after image need is established. sk-design's preflight currently requires every hero to have a real visual, and its content gate says a pure-text page is incomplete. Replace both absolute checks with an evidence-based rule: `signature role resolved = typographic | owned media | real product proof | generated asset | placeholder`; require source, subject, crop/aspect, mobile collapse, and reduced-motion behavior when media is present. Typography-only passes when the chosen page shape and brief rationale make typography the signature; a generic text-plus-gradient blob still fails. This is a high-value, low-to-medium-effort correction in `assets/interface-preflight-card.md`, `references/design-process/copy-and-mock-data.md`, and `procedures/aesthetic-direction.md`. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/hero-enrichment.md:5] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/hero-enrichment.md:11] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/hero-enrichment.md:39] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/assets/interface-preflight-card.md:44] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/copy-and-mock-data.md:161]

**The component cookbook's transferable pattern is `use when / don't confuse with / knobs / mobile behavior`, not paste-ready HTML/CSS.** Representative packets distinguish adjacent concepts (split diptych versus full-bleed photo; sticky-scroll stack versus numbered steps; product grid versus bento) and include responsive or interaction-specific constraints. sk-design's six private procedure cards currently operate at task level and do not provide compositional selection records. Add a private load-on-demand `structural-cookbook.md` reference owned by Interface, initially with a small set of semantic families—hero, evidence/proof section, navigation, footer, and commerce grid. Each card should contain `intent`, `adjacentPatternToReject`, `variationKnobs`, `stateRequirements`, `mobileCollapse`, and `proofGate`; implementation snippets remain downstream `sk-code` work. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/component-cookbook.md:14] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/components/h2-split-diptych.md:1] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/components/f2-sticky-scroll-stack.md:1] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/sk-design/014-hallmark-design-skill-research/external/hallmark/skills/hallmark/references/components/f6-product-card-grid.md:1] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/feature-catalog/procedure-cards/interface-procedure-card-inventory.md:18]

**The floating-nav recipe, preview block, and output contract improve handoff legibility when separated from implementation authority.** The floating-nav's durable ideas are constant layout geometry, one DOM owner, explicit property ownership, one timing family, and input/performance/state requirements; add these as a worked example in Interface's prototype-flow or Motion handoff rather than copying its exact 520 ms recipe. The preview block should become a concise `Design Receipt` inside the creation contract—page shape, system/theme source, signature/enrichment, regions, motion, proof status, provenance/assumptions—without CSS stamps or self-awarded “58/58” claims.
